layman_headline: Market fear and broad sell-off make it too risky to jump in right now — patience is the play.
layman_explanation: Almost everything in crypto dropped today, with many coins falling 8-10% in just 24 hours. Bitcoin slid below $64,000 after news of a U.S. strike on Iran rattled markets. Fear is the dominant emotion, and while that can eventually create buying opportunities, the selling hasn't shown signs of stopping yet. The best move right now is to wait for prices to stabilize before putting money to work.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines for now. Keep cash ready. If Hyperliquid drops to $56-57 and holds that level, it could become a strong entry. Don't chase anything falling this hard.
expert_reasoning: HYPE shows the best technical structure among all candidates with a chart bias of +1.1, price above 2 MAs, and a bullish marubozu candle pattern on the daily chart. However, the current -10% 24h and -12.5% 7d selloff is too aggressive to catch — RSI at 59 means there's room to fall further before becoming oversold. The geopolitical catalyst (Iran strike) creates unpredictable downside risk. BTC dominance at 56.2% is headwind for alts. ETH with RSI 35 is interesting but below all MAs with bearish structure. LDO's +19% 7d run looks extended. No candidate offers a clean risk/reward setup today — the prudent move is to wait for stabilization.
risk_factors: Geopolitical escalation (U.S.-Iran) could trigger another leg down across all risk assets
BTC dominance at 56.2% continues to crush alt performance — no sign of rotation
HYPE dropped 10% in 24h with RSI still at 59 — further correction likely before a bounce materializes
watch_list: HYPE price action around $56-58 support zone — a hold there with declining sell volume would signal entry
BTC reaction to $62,000 support — a break below would drag all alts further down
Fear & Greed index trajectory — currently 27 and improving from 25, but geopolitical shock could reverse this
market_context: Broad risk-off selling driven by a U.S. strike on Iran and Trump's China uncertainty. BTC below $64K with high dominance at 56.2% is crushing altcoins, and Fear & Greed at 27 shows the market hasn't found a floor yet.
context_banner: Geopolitical shock is driving indiscriminate selling — discipline means waiting for stabilization before deploying capital.

key_signals.description: U.S. strike on Iran and Trump China uncertainty are driving BTC below $64K and triggering broad risk-off selling across crypto.
Fear & Greed at 27 (Fear) — improving slightly from 25 but geopolitical escalation could reverse this trend, making it premature to buy.
No candidate is deeply oversold — BONK RSI 33 is closest but has terrible chart structure (-1.4 bias). ETH RSI 35 is interesting but below all MAs.
Citadel Securities $400M investment in Crypto.com at $20B valuation signals institutional confidence in crypto infrastructure, a medium-term positive for exchange tokens like HYPE.
No volume-confirmed breakout patterns detected — all candidates showing capitulation-style selling rather than accumulation.
